description: GrahaOS Capability Protocol manifest. Every stream opcode and channel type has an entry here; kernel and userspace derive matching tables from it via scripts/gen_manifest.py.

grahaos.io.v1.description: Phase 18 async I/O stream operations. Submitted via SYS_STREAM_SUBMIT to a stream created with this type_hash.

READ_VMO.description: Read len bytes from fd at offset into dest_vmo at dest_vmo_offset.

WRITE_VMO.description: Write len bytes from dest_vmo at dest_vmo_offset into fd at offset.

SENDMSG.description: Non-blocking send of inline payload into a channel referenced by fd_or_handle.

grahaos.io.completion.v1.description: Notification messages kernel sends on a stream's notify channel when a CQE is posted. Advisory only — reap from CQ VMO is authoritative.

grahaos.net.frame.v1.description: Phase 22 raw Ethernet frame slot notify (e1000d → netd / future rawframe peers). Carries (op, slot, length) tuples; payload lives in the shared rx/tx ring VMOs that e1000d clones to peers via VMO_CLONE_SHARED at ANNOUNCE time.

grahaos.net.service.v1.description: Phase 22 netd /sys/net/service per-client RPC channel. Carries libnet_msg headers (header.op_code) for HELLO / NET_QUERY / ICMP_ECHO / UDP_BIND / UDP_SENDTO / DNS_QUERY / TCP_OPEN / TCP_CLOSE / TCP_SEND / TCP_RECV / TCP_STATUS pairs.

grahaos.net.socket.v1.description: Phase 22 per-TCP-socket subordinate channel — same op-code namespace as service.v1 but scoped to a single open TCP cookie (negotiated in TCP_OPEN_RESP).

grahaos.net.accept.v1.description: Phase 22 named-channel accept channel for SYS_CHAN_PUBLISH/CONNECT. Internal kernel-side type used by rawnet.c to route a connector to the publisher's accept handler.

grahaos.blk.service.v1.description: Phase 23 ahcid /sys/blk/service per-client RPC channel. Carries blk_req_msg_t requests (BLK_OP_READ/WRITE/FLUSH/IDENTIFY) and blk_resp_msg_t responses. Connect handshake: client sends blk_connect_msg_t with shared DMA VMO handle.

grahaos.blk.list.v1.description: Phase 23 ahcid /sys/blk/list diagnostic channel. Used by /bin/blkctl to enumerate drives, sector counts, model strings, I/O counters.

SYS_SETRLIMIT.description: Set a per-task resource limit (mem pages, cpu ns/epoch, io bps). Pledge-gated by SYS_CONTROL regardless of direction (no self-lowering bypass). Out-of-spec: spec said 1081-1082; shifted +3 because Phase 19 consumed 1081-1083.

SYS_GETRLIMIT.description: Read a per-task resource limit. Default-granted pledge class. Returns 0 + writes to *value_out; -ESRCH / -EINVAL / -EFAULT on failure.

SYS_SPAWN_EX.description: Spawn with optional rlimit overrides. attrs.flags & SPAWN_ATTR_HAS_RLIMIT requires PLEDGE_SYS_CONTROL in addition to PLEDGE_SPAWN.

SYS_DRV_REGISTER.description: Phase 21: claim a userspace driver for a PCI device. R10 = drv_caps_t* receiving MMIO + IRQ + downstream + upstream cap handles + BAR phys/size + IRQ vector. Pledge gates: SYS_CONTROL plus the class-specific (NET_SERVER for class 0x02, STORAGE_SERVER for 0x01, INPUT_SERVER for 0x09). Returns 0 on success or -ENODEV/-EBUSY/-EPLEDGE.

SYS_DRV_IRQ_WAIT.description: Phase 21: block (or poll, timeout_ms=0) for IRQ messages from the SPSC ring associated with the supplied CAP_KIND_IRQ_CHANNEL token. Returns count copied (0..max_msgs) on success, 0 on timeout, -ESHUTDOWN on owner-death.

SYS_MMIO_VMO_CREATE.description: Phase 21: op=CREATE (default) maps a BAR-range as an MMIO VMO; phys+size validated against caller's owned BARs; returns CAP_KIND_MMIO_REGION cap_token. op=PHYS_QUERY (mode=1) resolves a VMO handle (CAP_KIND_VMO or _MMIO_REGION) and returns the physical backing of page_idx (used for descriptor-ring programming). Phase 21.1 extends SYS_VMO_MAP to accept CAP_KIND_MMIO_REGION.

SYS_CHAN_PUBLISH.description: Phase 22: claim a named slot in the kernel-side rawnet registry (kernel/net/rawnet.c). Subsequent SYS_CHAN_CONNECT calls with the same name route a connector to this publisher's accept handler. ASCII names, ≤ 64 bytes. AUDIT_CHAN_NAME_PUBLISH=39 emitted on success. Returns 0 / -EPERM (name claimed) / -EBADF (no free slots).

SYS_CHAN_CONNECT.description: Phase 22: connect to a published named channel by string name. Returns a fresh channel pair (write + read tokens) routed to the publisher; the publisher receives the peer-end via its accept handler. AUDIT_CHAN_NAME_CONNECT=40 emitted on success. -EBADF if no publisher / publisher dead. Used for /sys/net/rawframe (e1000d→netd) and /sys/net/service (netd→clients).

retired_syscalls._note: Phase 22 Stage F retired the in-kernel TCP/IP stack. The 5 entries below now return -ENOSYS (-38) after their pre-existing pledge gate. AUDIT_DEPRECATED_SYSCALL=17 is emitted on each call so audit_query surfaces unmigrated callers. Wrappers in user/syscalls.h are kept for pledgetest pledge-denial assertions; out-of-tree callers should migrate to libnet/libhttp.

SYS_HTTP_POST.replacement: http_post(&resp, url, body, body_len, content_type, timeout_ms)

audit_events._note: Phase 22 closeout: catalog of audit event ids (kernel/audit.h). Phase 22 added DEPRECATED_SYSCALL=17, CHAN_NAME_PUBLISH=39, CHAN_NAME_CONNECT=40.

phase22_libnet_msg_ops._note: Phase 22 libnet GCP message op codes. Each request op pairs with response op = request | 0x8000. All travel as inline_payload on grahaos.net.service.v1 / grahaos.net.socket.v1 channels.

HELLO.purpose: client → netd handshake; netd echoes capabilities

UDP_BIND.purpose: bind UDP socket on local port

TCP_OPEN.purpose: open TCP connection to (ip,port); returns socket cookie

TCP_RECV.purpose: drain bytes from per-socket rx ring; blocks/peeks

phase22_rawframe_ops._note: Phase 22 rawframe slot-notify ops on grahaos.net.frame.v1. e1000d publishes via SYS_CHAN_PUBLISH('/sys/net/rawframe', ...). RX_NOTIFY is e1000d → peer; TX_REQ is peer → e1000d.
